Book a walkthrough
Maximize the reach of your current / past research

You paid for your research.
Now leverage it to answer additional questions.

Pervasive Insights turns your brand's research history into a private insights portal… every study searchable in plain English, data searchable in synthesis across studies, plus a synthetic panel that mirrors your customers, calibrated to how they actually answered, that you can survey, interview, run group research with. Answers in about 90 seconds, for roughly a dollar a question.

Ask Your Synthetic Panel
Synthesis Across Datasets
"Where's the price ceiling on our premium fresh dog food?"Ask →
Answer · synthesized across 2 datasets

The price-sensitivity (Van Westendorp) ceiling lands at a median of $52 / bag, the wall where demand drops on price grounds. Acceptable corridor runs ~$34 good value to $46 getting pricey, and it holds across both studies.

Too cheap$22
Good value$34
Getting pricey$46
Too expensive$52
Sources: DATA Pet Owner Pricing · n=1,400 DATA Shopper Tracker Q1 · n=2,100

Sound familiar?

Your most valuable asset is asleep in a folder.

01

Years of commissioned studies sit in dozens of slide decks nobody reopens, the answers are in there, but you can't ask them.

02

You field a tracker once or twice a year and make decisions on instinct the other ten months.

03

Every new question means another six weeks and five figures to field... so most questions never get asked.

What if every study you've ever run could answer a new question in 90 seconds?

What it is

Two halves of one private portal.

We index any previous study you've commissioned (the last two years of projects up to your entire corpus), then build a synthetic panel of your buyers calibrated to it. One half answers from what you already know; the other lets you ask new questions of your customers, anytime, on demand.

Your own research

Everything you've already learned, made instantly usable

Research Library
Ask any past study in plain English; get an answer cited back to the slide.
Ask Your Data
Search and synthesize across every dataset at once, surface trends no single study could show.
Your synthetic panel

Your buyers, calibrated, ready for any question

One-on-One
Interview one customer, show a product, hear the honest reaction.
Surveys
Field to 10-200 calibrated respondents; structured results in minutes.
Focus Groups
A moderated room, agreement, pushback, quotable verbatims.
But does it actually answer like your customers?

That's the whole job. So we measure it, and show you.

~3-5pp
Client calibration runs ~3 to 5pp to human response, across all 19 areas of marketing.
100,000s
Built through observing hundreds of thousands of human respondents from five decades of commissioned research.
Every project updates
Re-calibrated with each project, each tracker wave. Compare your synthetic to human results in real time. Nothing hidden.

The compounding advantage

How this portal works in your world.

Say a design team has twelve concepts for a new seasonal line. The old way: pick two to three on instinct, test if the budget/timing allows, ship it, and hope. The new way: put all twelve in front of your calibrated panel on Monday morning.

01 · Screen cheap

Twelve to six by lunch

None come back as "the answer." Each one comes back human-shaped and segment-split: the gimmick scores low with your core buyers (measured, not guessed), a couple reveal splits worth arguing about, the rest are clear off-segment kills. 12 become 6 for about the cost of a sandwich.

02 · Iterate in minutes

Ten rounds if you want

Rework the survivors, re-drop them Tuesday, again Wednesday, as many passes as you like, because each round is minutes and pennies instead of six weeks and $50k.

03 · Test to crown

Resolve the close calls

The best three go to a real human test, where the genuinely close calls get settled and a winner is crowned with confidence.

04 · Compound forever

A tighter floor next time

That winner becomes a permanent marker on your map, so next season's concepts start from a tighter floor where this year there was open space.

It doesn't hand your team the answer. It hands them fifty affordable swings at finding it, and never forgets a swing they have already taken.

What a readout looks like

The same deliverables you commission today, in minutes.

A synthetic survey and a focus group, each run in about 90 seconds. Brands and products shown are fictitious and illustrative; your portal runs entirely on your own data.

Survey Results · n=50 · complete↓ CSV / PDF
Respondent distribution: Practical Owners 18Premium Seekers 14Value Shoppers 10Core Enthusiasts 8

Q1: Of these four fresh dog-food brands, which is closest to your go-to?

#1
Canine Choice
"Real food" / freshly prepared
34%n=17
#2
Happy Paws
Small-dog specialist
28%n=14
#3
Nature's Harvest
Fresh Bites, value-forward
22%n=11
#4
Cascade Provisions
Homestyle pouches
16%n=8
Q2: Why that brand? open ends by theme · click any comment to drill into a 1-on-1
Real-food ingredients I can recognize21 (42%)
"If I can't read the label, my dog doesn't eat it, that's why I switched." · Megan R. show more
"Real meat first, no fillers, that's the whole reason I pay more." · Carlos D. show more
"I want ingredients I'd recognize in my own kitchen, nothing I can't pronounce." · Priya N. show more
My dog actually finishes the bowl13 (26%)
"My picky shepherd cleans the bowl now, that sold me more than any claim." · Dana T. show more
"He used to leave half of it, with this he actually finishes." · Russell M. show more
"If my dog won't eat it the label doesn't matter, and this one she loves." · Helen K. show more
Vet recommended / digestion9 (18%)
"My vet recommended a fresh diet for his digestion and this fit." · Tom B. show more
"His coat got better within a month, that's real proof for me." · Aisha M. show more
"Fewer stomach issues since we switched, worth every penny." · Greg V. show more
Price / too expensive7 (14%)
"If it runs fifty-plus a bag I'm out, that's a lot for dog food." · Jason C. show more
"Way past what I'd pay when the store brand is fine for us." · Steven D. show more
"I'll spend more, but it has to clearly be worth it, not just nicer packaging." · Christine W. show more
Every comment is a real synthetic respondent, you can drill through and interview any of them. Brands and quotes are fictitious and illustrative.
Focus Group Report↓ PDF / CSV
Focus group report

Storm Sentry X · lightning detector

Regenerate report
3 groups · 9 participants · market-representative
TOPLINE: All 9 participants expressed interest, with no single feature universally winning. Real-time strike proximity drew 4 of 9, while distrust of unreliable phone weather apps was the dominant emotional driver across both segments.
Overall sentiment
Positive 33% Warm 13% Pragmatic 13% Cautious 20% Conflicted 7% Skeptical 7% Passionate 7%
By segment
Outdoor Enthusiast (6)
Safety-First Owner (3)
How interested would you be in this lightning detector?
Segment comparison
Outdoor Enthusiast (6)
Real-time strike proximity is what grabs me, I want a heads up before I head out on the water.
I check a weather app daily, but a dedicated device I'd actually trust more.
Wind speed and direction on the display catches my eye too.
Safety-First Owner (3)
The hyper-accurate local read is what matters, the apps are too general.
Getting caught off guard in a storm is exactly what this fixes.
Battery backup when the power is out is the real selling point.
"The lightning detection is what actually grabs me, right now I bounce between a couple weather apps and a device with my own readings is intriguing." · Marcus L.
"Apps are too general, having my own readings where I actually am would help me trust the forecast." · Jeffrey R.
Who's behind it

A full bench of senior insights professionals ready to assist.

SRMKALDPJT+Senior insights pros, decades each

Pervasive Insights is a full-service custom marketing-research and consulting firm in the Madison, Wisconsin area, established in 1979. No junior hand-offs: every engagement is run by senior professionals with decades in marketing consulting and research.

That team has delivered insights for everyone from the Fortune 100 to high-tech startups, across both qualitative and quantitative methods, measuring how consumers actually decide, from the retail shelf to in-home use. Your portal is built on that body of work, drawn from hundreds of thousands of human respondents across five decades of commissioned research. The panel isn't generic, it's yours.

Since 1979Madison, WIQual + QuantFortune 100 → startups
